data.title: Skyworks Solutions Inc expected to post earnings of $1.22 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Skyworks Solutions Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Irvine California-based company is expected to report a 3.9% increase in revenue to $940.849 million from $905.5 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 20 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on May 7 2025, for the period ended June 30, was for revenue between $920.00 million and $960.00 million.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Skyworks Solutions Inc is for earnings of $1.22 per share. The company's EPS guidance on May 7 2025, for the period ended June 30, was about USD1.24

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 21 "hold" and 6 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Skyworks Solutions Inc is $70.00, about 2.1% above its last closing price of $68.54

The company's guidance on May 7 2025 for the period ended June 30 was for gross profit margin between USD46% and USD47%.

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​

data.title: Wal-Mart de Mexico: President, CEO Ignacio Caride Resigns
American Well Corp expected to post a loss of $1.74 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• American Well Corp , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Boston Massachusetts-based company is expected to report a 1.1% increase in revenue to $63.482 million from $62.79 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 7 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on May 1 2025, for the period ended June 30, was for revenue between $62.00 million and $67.00 million.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for American Well Corp is for a loss of $1.74 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 7 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for American Well Corp is $10.00, about 29.9% above its last closing price of $7.01

The company's guidance on May 1 2025 for the period ended June 30 was for Earnings before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization of between 62 million and -10 million (USD).

data.title: Clearway Energy Inc expected to post earnings of 82 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Clearway Energy Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Princeton New Jersey-based company is expected to report a 17.7% increase in revenue to $430.947 million from $366 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Clearway Energy Inc is for earnings of 82 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 13 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Clearway Energy Inc is $36.00, about 15% above its last closing price of $30.59

data.title: Sun Capital Welcomes New Equity Partner Zeze Sun, Following $22.7M Strategic Acquisition of Irvine Medical Management
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 1, 2025 / In a key leadership move, following a strategic U.S. healthcare acquisition, Sun Capital Partners has officially named Zeze Sun as the new Equity Partner. The promotion comes after recognizing Sun for his instrumental role in the firm's recent M&A transaction with Irvine Medical Management, which is the parent company of Anaheim Surgery Center and A&C Urgent Care. Sun's leadership abilities and tenacious initiative helped spark a significant expansion of Sun Capital's overall operational footprint within the outpatient surgery sector.

"This transaction is a strategic inflection point not just for our portfolio, but for how we integrate legal, financial, and healthcare expertise into a unified growth platform," said Zeze Sun. "I'm honored to step into this expanded role at such a pivotal time."

Sun has also led the legal team that is responsible for full-spectrum regulatory diligence, deal structuring, and cross-border transaction execution, which ultimately has guided the firm through a complex and high-value healthcare acquisition.

Dr. Gary Y. Chen, MS, M.D., CEO of Anaheim Surgery Center Medical Group and Clinical Professor of Orthopedic Surgery at UCLA, who is formerly a microsurgery professor at Johns Hopkins University's renowned Curtis National Hand Center - also praised Sun's leadership record.

"Sun's strategic vision and deep understanding of the healthcare legal landscape was critical to making this acquisition both seamless and forward-thinking," said Dr. Gary Chen.

The acquisition aligns with Sun Capital‘s consistent strategy to scale high-performance assets in healthcare, with an emphasis on surgical efficiency, regulatory compliance, and digital integration. The capital firm is positioning itself to be a leading investor in the evolving U.S. outpatient medical landscape.

In recent years, Sun Capital has evolved into a family office that operates as a seasoned private equity firm, with significant committed capital and a diverse portfolio that spans across healthcare, legal services, accounting, media, and other regulated sectors. The firm primarily targets middle-market businesses with EBITDA between $20 million and $100 million, by applying an "operations-first" investment philosophy that blends financial discipline with hands-on operational expertise.

"Zeze Sun possesses a rare ability to execute complex transactions, while navigating diverse regulatory landscapes," said Jeff Conway, Senior Contributor for Forbes.

Sun's expertise will play a pivotal role in driving complex transactions and unlocking new value. With a global platform built on integration, execution, and long-term vision, Sun Capital is firmly positioned to shape the future of private equity investment.

To get in touch with Sun Partners, please email info@sunpartnerspc.com

SOURCE: Sun & Partners

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Health Care Down on Flight to Safety — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies fell as traders continued to flee the health-insurance subsector.

UnitedHealth Group's woes continued, with shares of the largest insurer by enrollees losing 5% to bring its losses for the year to date to 53%. UnitedHealth named Wayne DeVeydt as chief financial officer, replacing John Rex.

Pharmaceutical makers such as Pfizer, Eli Lilly and Merck were largely unshaken by the latest demands from President Trump to lower drug prices, with industry observers saying the new measures laid out in letters to 17 companies would either have modest impact or face legal challenges.

Some drugmakers rose as traders sought out defensive bets in light of a drop in jobs growth and some punitive tariff surprises.

German chemical and pharmaceutical giant Bayer raised it sales and earnings projections on a better-than-expected first half of the year for its pharmaceuticals business.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Financials Down on Weak Jobs Data — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions slid as weak jobs data and a new batch of punitive tariffs weighed on sentiment.

The CBOE volatility index, known as the "fear gauge" because it measures the cost of insuring stock portfolios, shot up by more than 20%, as traders braced for stagflation, following elevated inflation and weak jobs data.

In response to the jobs data, President Donald Trump said that he ordered his team to fire Bureau of Labor Statistics Commissioner Erika McEntarfer.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Industrials Down After Lackluster Jobs Growth, Weak Factory Data — Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies declined after lackluster jobs data and a slew of new tariff developments.

In one silver lining for cyclical companies, the weak jobs data could trigger a Federal Reserve rate cut in September.

"We still believe the macro environment is favorable for small [capitalization stocks], with Fed cuts on the horizon, high-yield spreads below 3%, and balance sheets in very good shape," said strategists at brokerage Jefferies, in a note to clients.

The Institute for Supply Management said its purchasing managers' index of manufacturing activity contracted to 48 in July from 49 in June, underscoring the slowing economic activity portrayed in the jobs report.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Independence Realty Trust Raised to Buy From Neutral by Compass Point
data.description: (21:39 GMT) Independence Realty Trust Price Target Raised to $24.00/Share From $22.00 by Compass Point

data.title: Mistral in talks with VC firms, MGX to raise funds at $10 billion valuation, FT reports
data.description:
French artificial intelligence startup Mistral is in talks with investors, venture capital firms and Abu Dhabi's MGX to raise $1 billion at a valuation of $10 billion, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The company launched in June Europe's first AI reasoning model, which uses logical thinking to create a response, as it tries to keep pace with American and Chinese rivals at the forefront of AI development.

The funding would accelerate the commercial rollout of Mistral's Le Chat chatbot and support continued development of its large language models, the report said.

MGX and Mistral did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

The startup raised 600 million euros in a Series B funding round that valued the company at 5.8 billion euros last year.

Industry observers consider Mistral as Europe's best-positioned AI company to rival Silicon Valley leaders, though the French firm has yet to achieve comparable market traction or revenue scale.

Mistral counts Nvidia , Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners among its investors.

data.title: Sadot Group Names Sansom CFO
data.description:
By Natalie Weger

Sadot Group named Paul Sansom chief financial officer of the global food supply chain company, effective Aug. 1.

Sansom succeeds Jennifer Black, who resigned as CFO on July 28. Black has agreed to serve as a consultant to the company for 60 days.

He was previously a member of the company's board and its audit committee. In line with his appointment as CFO, Sansom has resigned from those positions. He also serves as a board member and group chief financial officer of InterGen UK Limited, Sadot said.

Sansom will receive an annual base salary of $190,000 for the first six months of employment. At the end of that period, Sansom will be subject to a base salary increase to a minimum of $380,000 a year, according to the company.

Write to Natalie Weger at natalie.weger@wsj.com

data.title: STARTENGINE CROWDFUNDING, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc., a leading platform for equity crowdfunding and private offerings, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational improvements. The report highlights the company's robust growth in revenue and profitability, driven by the success of its new product lines and strategic operational adjustments.

Financial Highlights

StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc. reported impressive financial results for the period, reflecting substantial growth and improved profitability:

  • Total Revenues: $70.18 million, a remarkable increase of 225% from the same period in 2024, primarily driven by the success of StartEngine Private offerings.
  • Gross Profit: $22.31 million, an increase of $11.63 million compared to the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability.
  • Operating Income: $4.96 million, a substantial improvement from an operating loss of $6.98 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting effective cost management and increased revenue.
  • Net Income: $4.99 million, a turnaround from a net loss of $7.04 million in the same period in 2024, demonstrating strong financial performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.00, reflecting the company's improved earnings position compared to a loss per share in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

The company's business performance has been bolstered by strategic initiatives and new product launches:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported significant growth in its StartEngine Private product line, which became the largest revenue generator in 2025. This product line involves providing accredited investors the opportunity to purchase membership interests in funds that own shares of venture capital-backed, late-stage private companies.
  • Revenue Segments: Regulation Crowdfunding platform fees saw a slight increase due to higher raise totals for issuers within the period, while Regulation A commissions increased significantly due to higher amounts raised in offerings.
  • Revenue Segments: StartEngine Premium revenue increased due to a shift in payment strategy, more issuers utilizing the services, and a price increase from $15,000 to $17,000.
  • Revenue Segments: StartEngine Secure revenue increased due to a change in billing strategy to a tiered service annual contract.
  • New Product Launches: The company launched 'StartEngine Private' in August 2023, which has quickly become the largest source of revenue, indicating a successful new product introduction.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on the StartEngine Private product line as a major revenue source, although growth may be limited by factors such as stock availability and demand.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates an increase in Regulation A and Crowdfunding revenues as raises continue to disburse, and it plans to complete the transition of hosting new Regulation Crowdfunding issuances with its broker-dealer entity by the end of 2025.
  • Operational Adjustments: At the end of 2024, the company reduced its workforce by 25% to remain competitive and reduce costs, aiming for profitability in 2025.
  • Operational Adjustments: The company is leveraging more technology to improve process efficiency and scale the business, as evidenced by an increase in software expenses.

SEC Filing: STARTENGINE CROWDFUNDING, INC. [ STGC ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025
